Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), strong password (e.g., Passw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe), address (e.g., Via Roma 1, 30175 Venezia)
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration page.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter strong password.
4. Re-enter the same password.
5. Enter full name.
6. Enter address.
7. Click "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message appears. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: Successful User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com), correct password (e.g., Passw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. Open the login page.
2. Enter registered email address.
3. Enter correct password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully, and the app redirects to the home screen or profile page.

Title: Failed User Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com), incorrect password (e.g., wrongpass)
Test Steps:
1. Open the login page.
2. Enter registered email address.
3. Enter incorrect password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Invalid email or password." User remains on the login page.

Title: Add New Incident Report with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Valid incident type (e.g., Theft), valid location (e.g., Piazza San Marco), description (e.g., "Suspicious activity observed"), timestamp (current date/time)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Add Report" section.
2. Select incident type from dropdown (e.g., Theft).
3. Specify location (e.g., Piazza San Marco).
4. Enter description.
5. Click "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Incident report is saved successfully, and a confirmation message appears. Report is visible in the user’s report history.

Title: Specify Incident Type for New Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the "Add Report" page.
Test Data: Predefined incident types (e.g., Theft, Vandalism, Noise, Illegal Parking)
Test Steps:
1. Open the "Add Report" page.
2. Click the dropdown menu for incident type.
3. Select an incident type (e.g., Vandalism).
4. Verify the selected type is displayed.
Expected Result: Selected incident type is saved and displayed correctly in the report form.

Title: Specify Location for New Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the "Add Report" page.
Test Data: Valid location (e.g., Via Roma 1, 30175 Venezia), GPS coordinates (if applicable)
Test Steps:
1. Open the "Add Report" page.
2. Enter location details (e.g., Via Roma 1).
3. Optionally, enter GPS coordinates if required.
4. Verify the location is displayed on a map (if applicable).
Expected Result: Location is saved and displayed correctly in the report form. Map (if available) shows the selected location.

Title: Receive Notification for Nearby Incident Reported by Another User
Preconditions: User is logged in and has enabled notifications. Another user has reported an incident within a predefined radius (e.g., 500 meters).
Test Data: Incident reported by another user (e.g., Theft at Piazza San Marco), user’s location set to within 500 meters of the incident.
Test Steps:
1. Another user reports an incident (e.g., Theft at Piazza San Marco).
2. Ensure the reporting user’s location is within 500 meters of the test user’s location.
3. Wait for notification to appear.
Expected Result: User receives a real-time notification about the incident, including type, location, and timestamp. Notification appears in the app’s notification center.

Title: View User Profile Information
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: User profile data (e.g., full name, email, address, registration date)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Profile" section.
2. Click "View Profile" button.
Expected Result: User’s profile information is displayed correctly, including full name, email, address, and registration date.

Title: Modify User Profile with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the profile page.
Test Data: Updated profile data (e.g., new address: Via Garibaldi 2, new phone number: +393451234567)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Profile" section.
2. Click "Edit Profile" button.
3. Update address field with new address.
4. Update phone number field with new number.
5. Click "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Profile is updated successfully, and a confirmation message appears. Updated data is reflected in the profile view.

Title: Modify Existing Incident Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one reported incident.
Test Data: Existing incident report (e.g., Theft at Piazza San Marco), updated description (e.g., "Suspicious person seen near ATM"), updated location (e.g., Via Roma 2)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Reports" section.
2. Select an existing report to modify.
3. Update the description field with new details.
4. Update the location if necessary.
5. Click "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Incident report is updated successfully, and a confirmation message appears. Updated details are reflected in the report history.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: App is installed on an Android device (version ≥ 8.1)
Test Data: Valid username, password, name, surname, gender, date of birth, phone number, email
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. User is automatically redirected to the registration screen.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid data: Username, Password, Name, Surname, Gender, Date of Birth, Phone, Email.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: Account is created in the database, and the user is redirected to the main application screen.

Title: User Registration with Missing Required Field
Preconditions: App is installed on an Android device (version ≥ 8.1)
Test Data: Valid data except for one missing required field (e.g., email)
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. User is redirected to the registration screen.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid data but leave one required field (e.g., email) empty.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the missing field and prevents account creation.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User has an existing account and the app is installed
Test Data: Valid email and password used during registration
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Enter the registered email and password.
3. Press the "Accedi" (Login) button.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the main application screen.

Title: Login with Incorrect Credentials
Preconditions: User has an existing account and the app is installed
Test Data: Valid email but incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Enter the registered email and an incorrect password.
3. Press the "Accedi" (Login) button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating invalid credentials and prevents login.

Title: Login with Non-Existent Account
Preconditions: App is installed
Test Data: Non-registered email and any password
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Enter a non-registered email and any password.
3. Press the "Accedi" (Login) button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the account does not exist and prevents login.

Title: Redirect to Registration for Unregistered User
Preconditions: App is installed, user does not have an account
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Attempt to log in without an account (leave fields empty or enter invalid data).
3. System detects no account exists.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the registration screen with a message prompting registration.

Title: Successful Emergency Report Submission
Preconditions: User is logged in and an emergency occurs in real life
Test Data: Valid type of emergency (from predefined list), valid location (manual or GPS)
Test Steps:
1. User taps the "Segnala Emergenza" (Report Emergency) button.
2. User is redirected to the emergency report form.
3. User selects a type of emergency from the predefined list.
4. User specifies the location of the emergency either manually or via GPS.
5. User submits the form.
Expected Result: Emergency report is saved in the database with the specified type and location.

Title: Emergency Report with Missing Type
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Valid location but no type selected
Test Steps:
1. User taps the "Segnala Emergenza" (Report Emergency) button.
2. User is redirected to the emergency report form.
3. User skips selecting the type of emergency.
4. User specifies the location of the emergency.
5. User attempts to submit the form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the type of emergency is required and prevents submission.

Title: Emergency Report with Missing Location
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Valid type but no location provided
Test Steps:
1. User taps the "Segnala Emergenza" (Report Emergency) button.
2. User is redirected to the emergency report form.
3. User selects a type of emergency.
4. User skips specifying the location.
5. User attempts to submit the form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the location is required and prevents submission.

Title: Emergency Report with GPS Location
Preconditions: User is logged in, GPS is enabled on the device
Test Data: Valid type of emergency
Test Steps:
1. User taps the "Segnala Emergenza" (Report Emergency) button.
2. User is redirected to the emergency report form.
3. User selects a type of emergency.
4. User allows GPS to automatically detect the location.
5. User submits the form.
Expected Result: Emergency report is saved in the database with the GPS-detected location.

Title: User Receives Notifications for Nearby Emergencies
Preconditions: An emergency is reported near the user’s home address
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. An emergency is reported near the user’s registered home address.
2. The server sends a notification to the user’s device.
3. User receives the notification and taps on it.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the screen displaying the emergency details.

Title: User Receives All Notifications if No Home Address is Registered
Preconditions: An emergency is reported anywhere, user has no home address registered
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. An emergency is reported anywhere.
2. The server sends a notification to the user’s device.
3. User receives the notification and taps on it.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the screen displaying the emergency details.

Title: User Ignores Notification
Preconditions: User receives a notification
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User receives a notification for an emergency.
2. User swipes the notification away or dismisses it.
Expected Result: Notification is cleared from the device, and no further action is taken.

Title: Create New User Account
Preconditions: User opens the app for the first time
Test Data: Valid name, surname, date of birth, email, phone number, address
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User selects the registration option.
3. User fills in the registration form with valid data.
4. User submits the form.
Expected Result: User account is created and added to the database. User is redirected to the app home screen.

Title: View Nearby Report on Map
Preconditions: User is within the range of a report
Test Data: Existing report within user’s location range
Test Steps:
1. User is logged in and located within the range of a report.
2. User selects the report marker on the map.
3. User is redirected to the report view screen.
Expected Result: Report details are displayed correctly.

Title: View Report Details
Preconditions: User has selected a report on the map
Test Data: Existing report
Test Steps:
1. User is on the report view screen.
2. User scrolls through the report content.
Expected Result: All report information (title, description, location, etc.) is displayed correctly.

Title: Modify Own Report
Preconditions: User has created a report and is logged in
Test Data: Existing report created by the user
Test Steps:
1. User selects a report they created on the map.
2. User clicks the "Modify Report" option.
3. User is redirected to the report modification screen.
4. User edits report details (e.g., title, description).
5. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: Report is updated in the database with the new details.

Title: View User Profile
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in
Test Data: Existing user profile with personal data and addresses
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Profile" option from the menu.
2. User views the profile screen.
Expected Result: User’s personal data (name, surname, date of birth, phone, email) and addresses are displayed correctly.

Title: Modify User Profile (Non-Email Fields)
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an existing profile
Test Data: Valid updated phone number, address, and personal data (excluding email)
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Profile" option from the menu.
2. User clicks the "Modify Profile" option.
3. User edits non-email fields (e.g., phone number, address).
4. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: Updated profile data is saved in the database. Changes are reflected in the profile view.

Title: Real-Time User Location Update
Preconditions: User has an active GPS connection and is logged in
Test Data: User moves to a new location
Test Steps:
1. User moves to a new location while the app is open or in the background.
2. App periodically updates user location.
Expected Result: User’s location is updated in the database via API. Nearby reports are recalculated and displayed on the map.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User account exists in the system with valid credentials (username/email and password).
Test Data: Valid username/email: "user@example.com", Valid password: "SecurePass123"
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Navigate to the login screen.
3. Enter the valid username/email in the username/email field.
4. Enter the valid password in the password field.
5. Tap the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the home/profile screen.

Title: Successful Logout from Account
Preconditions: User is currently logged in to the application.
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and ensure the user is logged in.
2. Navigate to the profile or settings screen.
3. Tap the "Logout" button.
Expected Result: User is logged out successfully and redirected to the login screen.

Title: Add a New Report with Predefined Type
Preconditions: User is logged in to the application.
Test Data: Report type: "Traffic Issue", Location: "Via Roma 123", Description: "Pothole on the road"
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and ensure the user is logged in.
2. Navigate to the "Add Report" screen.
3. Select a predefined report type (e.g., "Traffic Issue").
4. Enter the location of the report (e.g., "Via Roma 123").
5. Enter a description of the report (e.g., "Pothole on the road").
6. Tap the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Report is successfully added and displayed in the user's report history or the community feed.

Title: View All Community Reports
Preconditions: At least one report exists in the system.
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and ensure the user is logged in.
2. Navigate to the "Reports" or "Community Feed" screen.
3. Ensure the list of reports is displayed.
Expected Result: All community reports are successfully displayed in a list or feed format.

Title: Edit an Existing Report
Preconditions: User has added at least one report and is logged in.
Test Data: Existing report ID: "REP001", Updated description: "Large pothole causing damage to vehicles"
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and ensure the user is logged in.
2. Navigate to the "My Reports" or "Manage Reports" screen.
3. Select the existing report (e.g., "REP001").
4. Tap the "Edit" button.
5. Modify the description to "Large pothole causing damage to vehicles".
6. Tap the "Save" button.
Expected Result: Report is successfully updated with the new description.

Title: Verify Database Security for Sensitive User Data
Preconditions: User account exists with sensitive data (e.g., email, phone number, residence).
Test Data: Sensitive data: Email: "user@example.com", Phone: "3456789012", Residence: "Via Roma 123"
Test Steps:
1. Add a new user account with sensitive data (email, phone, residence).
2. Log in to the account.
3. Attempt to access or modify sensitive data without proper authentication (e.g., via direct database access or unauthorized API calls).
Expected Result: Access to sensitive data is denied, and data remains secure and unaltered.

Title: Verify Application Compatibility with Android 8.1+
Preconditions: Android emulator or device with Android 8.1 or higher is set up.
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device/emulator with Android 8.1.
2. Attempt to open and use the application.
3. Verify all primary functionalities (login, report addition, report viewing).
Expected Result: Application installs and runs successfully on Android 8.1 without crashes or errors.

Title: Verify Application Response Time for Report Submission
Preconditions: User is logged in and connected to the internet.
Test Data: Report type: "Noise Complaint", Location: "Via Roma 123", Description: "Loud music at night"
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and navigate to the "Add Report" screen.
2. Enter report details (type, location, description).
3. Measure the time taken from tapping "Submit" to the confirmation screen appearing.
Expected Result: Report submission completes within 3 seconds or less.

Title: Verify Clean and Intuitive UI Design
Preconditions: Application is installed and launched
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Navigate through all primary screens (e.g., home, map, event list).
3. Observe the layout, color scheme, and text readability.
Expected Result: The UI is visually clean, intuitive, and free of clutter. All interactive elements are easily identifiable and accessible.

Title: Verify Firebase Database Integration
Preconditions: Internet connection is active
Test Data: Sample user data (e.g., event details)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to connect to Firebase database.
2. Perform a write operation (e.g., add a test event).
3. Perform a read operation (e.g., retrieve the test event).
Expected Result: The database connection is established successfully. Data is written and retrieved without errors.

Title: Verify Internet Connectivity Requirement
Preconditions: Application is launched
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the device.
2. Attempt to perform any database-related operation (e.g., load events).
Expected Result: The application displays an error message indicating the need for an active internet connection.

Title: Verify GPS Functionality for Location Tracking
Preconditions: GPS is enabled on the device
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Navigate to the map screen.
3. Observe if the user’s current location is displayed on the map.
Expected Result: The user’s location is accurately displayed on the map with minimal delay.

Title: Verify GPS Error Handling
Preconditions: GPS is disabled on the device
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Navigate to the map screen.
Expected Result: The application prompts the user to enable GPS or displays an error message indicating GPS is required.

Title: Verify Secure Data Transmission
Preconditions: Internet connection is active
Test Data: Sample sensitive user data (e.g., login credentials)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to transmit sensitive data to the database.
2. Use a network monitoring tool to verify encryption in transit.
Expected Result: Data is transmitted using secure protocols (e.g., HTTPS) and is encrypted during transit.

Title: Verify Data Encryption at Rest
Preconditions: Data is stored in Firebase database
Test Data: Sample sensitive user data
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve stored data from the database.
2. Verify if the data is encrypted in the database.
Expected Result: Sensitive data is stored in an encrypted format within the database.

Title: Verify Reasonable Operation Response Time
Preconditions: Internet and GPS are active
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Perform a database read/write operation (e.g., add or retrieve an event).
2. Measure the time taken for the operation to complete.
Expected Result: All operations complete within a reasonable time frame (e.g., < 2 seconds for read/write).

Title: Verify Compatibility with Android 8.1+
Preconditions: Application is installed on a device with Android 8.1+
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on a device running Android 8.1 or higher.
2. Perform basic operations (e.g., navigate screens, load data).
Expected Result: The application runs without crashes or compatibility issues.

Title: Verify Minimum Storage Requirement
Preconditions: Device has < 300 MB free storage
Test Data: None
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to install the application on a device with insufficient storage (< 300 MB).
Expected Result: The installation fails with an error message indicating insufficient storage space.

Title: Verify Data Deletion on Uninstall
Preconditions: Application is installed and contains user data
Test Data: Sample user data
Test Steps:
1. Uninstall the application from the device.
2. Verify if all application-related data is deleted.
Expected Result: All user data associated with the application is permanently deleted upon uninstall.

Title: First-time user account creation
Preconditions: Application installed correctly on Android device (OS 8.1+), device has internet connection
Test Data: Valid email, phone number, password, residence
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time
2. Select the option to create a new account
3. Enter valid email, phone number, password, and residence
4. Confirm registration by tapping the "Register" button
Expected Result: Account is successfully created, user is redirected to the app’s main screen

Title: Update user phone number and residence
Preconditions: User is logged in with an existing account
Test Data: Updated phone number, updated residence
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile/account settings section
2. Select the option to edit phone number and residence
3. Enter new phone number and residence
4. Confirm changes by tapping the "Save" button
Expected Result: Phone number and residence are updated successfully, confirmation message appears

Title: Submit a predefined report
Preconditions: User is logged in, user knows the app’s rules
Test Data: Valid report type (e.g., "Noise Complaint"), location coordinates, description
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Submit Report" section
2. Select a predefined report type (e.g., "Noise Complaint")
3. Enter location coordinates (via GPS or manual input)
4. Add a description if required
5. Confirm submission by tapping the "Submit" button
Expected Result: Report is created in the system, user sees a confirmation message, new entry appears in the database

Title: View all community reports on map
Preconditions: User is logged in, at least one report exists in the system
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Community Reports" section
2. Select the map view option
Expected Result: Map displays all community reports with markers, reports are clickable to show details

Title: User login to the application
Preconditions: User has a registered account, user is not currently logged in
Test Data: Valid registered email/phone and password
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen
2. Enter registered email/phone and password
3. Tap the "Login" button
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, redirected to the app’s main screen

Title: User logout from the application
Preconditions: User is currently logged in
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile/settings section
2. Select the "Logout" option
3. Confirm logout if prompted
Expected Result: User is logged out, redirected to the login screen, cannot access reports or comments

Title: Edit or delete a user-submitted report
Preconditions: User is logged in, user has submitted at least one report
Test Data: Existing report ID, updated report details (if editing)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Reports" section
2. Select the report to edit or delete
3. For editing: Modify report details (e.g., description, location)
4. For deletion: Select the "Delete" option and confirm
Expected Result: Report is updated/deleted successfully, changes reflect in the system and user’s report list
